text: Tesla FSD 14.3.4 rolling out now https://t.co/Lbtx2Yeo6i
ai_summary: Elon Musk announced the rollout of Tesla FSD (Full Self-Driving) version 14.3.4, indicating a new software update deployment to Tesla vehicles. This represents Tesla's continued iteration and improvement of its autonomous driving capabilities, a core product differentiator and long-term value driver for the company. The insight reflects Tesla's ongoing execution in advancing self-driving technology through rapid software deployment cycles.
embed_html:
Tesla FSD 14.3.4 rolling out now pic.twitter.com/Lbtx2Yeo6i

— Elon Musk (@elonmusk) June 11, 2026

text: Within ~5 years, probably ~5 times as many satellites as rest of world https://t.co/EODE2lcbM0
summary: Within ~5 years, probably ~5 times as many satellites as rest of world
ai_summary: Elon projects that Starlink will deploy approximately 5 times more satellites than all other companies combined within ~5 years, reflecting SpaceX's aggressive expansion of its satellite internet constellation. This statement highlights Starlink's market dominance strategy and SpaceX's manufacturing/launch capability scaling, demonstrating how vertical integration and reusable rocket technology enable rapid satellite deployment for global broadband coverage.
embed_html:
Within ~5 years, probably ~5 times as many satellites as rest of world https://t.co/EODE2lcbM0

— Elon Musk (@elonmusk) June 13, 2026

text: @PalmerLuckey @paranoidream That is what they told me
summary: @PalmerLuckey @paranoidream That is what they told me

parent.text: "There are only 25 satellites launched a year, every year, and that's not going to change." Ryhmes with the current defense ecosystem.
parent.name: Palmer Luckey
parent.embed_html:
"There are only 25 satellites launched a year, every year, and that's not going to change."

Ryhmes with the current defense ecosystem. https://t.co/PfH7cha3Ew

— Palmer Luckey (@PalmerLuckey) June 13, 2026

ai_summary: Palmer Luckey references an entrenched defense industry constraint that satellite launches are artificially limited to ~25 per year, which Elon confirms he encountered as an obstacle. This illustrates a critical business insight for SpaceX: breaking into aerospace requires overcoming not just technical challenges, but regulatory and incumbent-protection barriers that artificially constrain market capacity. The lesson is that transformative companies must identify and challenge these artificial limitations to unlock exponential growth in their addressable market.
embed_html:
That is what they told me

— Elon Musk (@elonmusk) June 13, 2026
